responseText:
**Rivian R1 (“ToughNut”) – 10‑Day Activity Overview (Feb 27 – Mar 8 2026)** | Day | Key Driving Highlights | Key Charging Highlights | Notes | |-----|------------------------|--------------------------|-------| | **Feb 27** | • 93.6 mi long‑haul to Bennington, VT (energy = 50 kWh, 73 % eff.)
• 58.1 mi “cross‑state” to Ludlow, VT (27 % battery drop, 82 % eff.)
• 20.3 mi efficient drive (96 % eff.) on Mar 8 (see below) | • 8.5 kWh in Cornwall (late‑night, 10 kW) – cost $0.88
• 31.2 kWh DC‑fast at Cornwall (March 4–6) – cost $0 (free‑charge network) | First day of the window – a mix of short hops and a major 93‑mile trip. | | **Feb 28** | • 6.8 mi local run (52 % eff.)
• 5.8 mi to Ludlow (39 % eff.) | • 4.5 kWh in Cornwall (10 kW) – cost $0.25 | Light day; mostly local errands. | | **Mar 1** | • 123.5 mi multi‑state run (75 % eff.) – **longest drive** in the period
• 10 mi brief trip to Canaan (24 % eff.) | • 8.5 kWh at North Canaan, VT (212 kW DC‑fast) – cost $17.73 | Heavy‑traffic day with the longest single drive. | | **Mar 2** | • 71.9 mi cross‑state drive to Dover, VT (62 % eff.)
• 8 mi short trip to Great Barrington, MA | • 7.9 kWh at Dover (7 kW) – cost $0 (overnight) | Long‑range drive, no major charging stops. | | **Mar 3** | • 77.2 mi long trip to Stowe, VT (64 % eff.)
• 53.5 mi long‑haul to West Lebanon (56 % eff.) | • 78.6 kWh DC‑fast at Holyoke, MA (215 kW) – cost $0
• 63.2 kWh DC‑fast at Lebanon, NH (218 kW) – cost $0 | Overnight supercharger use, high‑power fast charges. | | **Mar 4** | • 114.5 mi long drive to Holyoke, MA (67 % eff.)
• 55.7 mi to Lebanon, NH (81 % eff.) | • 78.6 kWh DC‑fast at Holyoke, MA – cost $0 (free network)
• 63.2 kWh DC‑fast at Lebanon, NH – cost $0 | Heavy day with two supercharger sessions, both free. | | **Mar 5** | • 17.7 mi local run to Waterbury, VT (92 % eff.)
• 8 mi to Stowe, VT (24 % eff.) | • 39.6 kWh charging in Cornwall (10 kW) – cost $4.10
• 31.3 kWh overnight charge (10 kW) – cost $3.24 | Two significant charges; a mix of moderate and high‑energy sessions. | | **Mar 6** | • 14.9 mi drive to Cornwall, CT (65 % eff.)
• 3.4 mi to Sharon, CT (50 % eff.) | • 2.5 kWh quick charge in Cornwall (10 kW) – cost $0.25
• 14.5 kWh overnight charge (10 kW) – cost $1.50 | Light day, just a few short trips and a long overnight charge. | | **Mar 7** | • 20.3 mi efficient drive (96 % eff.)
• 3.2 mi local trip (58 % eff.) | • 1.7 kWh quick charge in Dover (6 kW) – cost $0 | Minimal driving and charging; battery topped up slightly. | | **Mar 8** | • 20.3 mi **most efficient** drive (96 % eff.)
• 14.9 mi local hop (65 % eff.)
• 3.4 mi to Sharon (50 % eff.) | • 2.5 kWh quick charge in Cornwall (10 kW) – cost $0.25 | End of the window, high‑efficiency day with short local runs. | ### Summary of the 10‑Day Period | Metric | Value | |--------|-------| | **Total distance** | 1 077.9 mi | | **Number of drives** | 74 | | **Average drive efficiency** | 64 % | | **Longest single drive** | 123.5 mi (Mar 1) | | **Most efficient drive** | 96 % (20.3 mi on Mar 8) | | **Total energy added** | 669.7 kWh | | **Number of charges** | 17 | | **Total charging cost** | **≈ $54.11** | | **Longest charge** | 78.6 kWh (Mar 4, Holyoke supercharger) | | **Highest‑power supercharger** | 218 kW (Mar 4, Lebanon, NH) | **Take‑away narrative** Over the 10‑day span the Rivian R1 moved over 1,000 mi, driving a mix of short errands and a handful of long cross‑state trips (the longest being 123.5 mi on March 1). Driving efficiency averaged 64 %, but the most efficient single trip—96 %—was a 20 mi run on March 8. Charging activity was dominated by overnight and long‑duration sessions in Cornwall, CT, and a series of free, high‑power DC‑fast supercharger stops in Holyoke (215 kW) and Lebanon (218 kW) on March 4–6, which provided over 150 kWh in two sessions alone. Total energy added over the period was 669.7 kWh, costing roughly $54.11, with most of the cost spread across multiple moderate‑power 10 kW chargers. The data shows consistent usage of the vehicle across the two‑week window, with both high‑frequency local drives and a few long‑haul trips, all supported by a robust charging schedule that kept the battery range above 70 % throughout.
